title: SACRAMENTO – For far too many Californians who have no choice but to go to civil court to resolve a dispute, the court system’s high filing fees make getting through the front door

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The court system’s high filing fees limit access to civil court for financially-pressed Californians.",
    "Senator Josh Becker introduced SB 355 to expand eligibility criteria for low-income Californians seeking a waiver of filing fees.",
    "The provisions of SB 355 were absorbed into AB 199, a state budget-related bill that took effect on June 30, 2022.",
    "California’s high cost of living was not accounted for in the original qualification threshold for filing fee waivers.",
    "The income threshold for filing fee waivers was raised from 125% to 200% of the federal poverty level as part of AB 199.",
    "Fees for responding to lawsuits can range from $30 for small claims cases to over $1,000 for complex civil cases.",
    "Only allowing individuals who can afford filing fees to access courts is considered a denial of justice."
  ]
}
```
